q:
scratch
 He ___ his head.

hide
 She ___ behind a tree.
a: She is hiding behind a tree.

q:
wave

cross

I need to eat something soon. (get / hungry)
 I ___ hungry.

We need to leave soon. (get / late)
 It ___ late.

They don't need their car any more. (try / sell / it)
 They ___ it.
a: They are trying to sell it.

q:

 The company ___ money.

 They ___.

 It ___ to rain.

 ___

 ___

q:

 ___

Who are those people? (what / they / do?)
 ___

I heard you started a new job. (you / enjoy / it?)
 ___

 ___
a: Why are you walking so fast?

q:
You can turn off the radio. (I / listen / to it)
 I ___ to it.
a: I am not listening to it.

q:
Kate is on holiday with friends. (She / have / a great time)
 She ___ a great time.
a: She is having a great time.

q:
Andrew started evening classes recently. (He / learn / Japanese)
 He ___ Japanese.

Paul and Sarah have had an argument. (they / speak / to one another)
 They ___ to one another.
a: They are not speaking to one another.

q:
The situation is already very bad. (it / get / worse)
 It ___ worse.

Tim has taken the day off. (work / today)
 Tim ___ today.

I ___ for Sophie. (I / look)
 Do you know where she is?

The washing machine has been repaired. (It / work / now)
 It ___ now.

They ___ a new hospital. (They / build)
 It will be finished next year.
a: They are building a new hospital.

q:
Ben is a student, but he's not very happy. (He / enjoy / his course)
 He ___ his course.
a: He is not enjoying his course.

q:
The weather ___ . (change)
 Look at those clouds.

Dan has been in the same job for a long time. (He / start / to get bored)
 He ___ to get bored with it.
a: He is starting to get bored with it.
